field_updates.title: Kolkata Government Building Fire
field_updates.accused_gap: No named accused, arrests, or prosecution details were identified in the frozen source set.
field_updates.summary: The Hindu reported that around 4,000 electronic voting machines were gutted when a nine-storey government building in Kolkata's Alipore area caught fire on 10 June 2026. The article said officials flagged a possibility of sabotage, an FIR was lodged, a four-member SIT was formed, and forensic experts were collecting samples to determine whether the fire was accidental or deliberate.

compensation.paid_note: No compensation reporting was frozen for this draft.

timeline.event: The Hindu reported that around 4,000 electronic voting machines were gutted after a government building in Kolkata's Alipore area caught fire on Wednesday, 10 June 2026.

The article said the nine-storey building in Alipore housed the South 24 Parganas Zila Parishad, the local District Forest Officer's office, and Sarva Shiksha Abhiyaan, and that the EVMs were stored on the ninth floor.
The article said an FIR was lodged, a four-member special investigating team was formed, and forensic experts collected samples to determine whether the fire was accidental or deliberate.

notes: The draft stays within the frozen The Hindu article and does not invent any casualty or accused details.
Deaths and injuries remain null because the source does not report casualty counts.
The FIR, SIT, and forensic-probe language is source-backed and does not overstate legal action.
